message.content:
{
  "assessment_framework": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry - 7 Marks",
      "Unit 2: Structure of Atom - 9 Marks",
      "Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks",
      "Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks",
      "Unit 5: Chemical Thermodynamics - 9 Marks",
      "Unit 6: Equilibrium - 7 Marks",
      "Unit 7: Redox Reactions - 4 Marks",
      "Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks",
      "Unit 9: Hydrocarbons - 10 Marks",
      "Total Theory Marks: 70"
    ],
    "question_paper_design": [
      "Time Duration: 3 Hours",
      "Total Marks: 70 (Theory)",
      "Formative Assessment Topics (No Summative Weightage): s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State"
    ],
    "competency_weightage": [
      "Measurement and Uncertainty in Measurement",
      "Stoichiometry and Stoichiometric Calculations",
      "Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model",
      "Periodic Trends and Electronic Configuration",
      "Chemical Bonding Theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital)",
      "Thermodynamic Terms, Enthalpy Change, Spontaneity, Gibbs Energy",
      "Equilibrium Constants, Reaction Quotient, Factors Affecting Equilibria",
      "Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes",
      "Organic Nomenclature, Isomerism, Reaction Mechanisms",
      "Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties"
    ],
    "bloom_levels": [
      "Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law",
      "Understanding: Bohr's Model, VSEPR Theory, Bonding Types",
      "Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, pH Determination, Titration",
      "Analysis: Electronic Configuration Trends, Equilibrium Shifts",
      "Evaluation: Purification Methods, Project Investigations"
    ],
    "internal_assessment": [
      "Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (No marks in final theory exam)",
      "Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (No marks in final theory exam)"
    ],
    "practical_assessment": {
      "total_marks": 30,
      "scheme": [
        "Volumetric Analysis: 8 Marks",
        "Salt Analysis: 8 Marks",
        "Content Based Experiment: 6 Marks",
        "Project Work: 4 Marks",
        "Class Record and Viva: 4 Marks"
      ],
      "syllabus_areas": [
        "Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing, Boring)",
        "Characterization and Purification (Melting/Boiling Point, Crystallization)",
        "Experiments based on pH (Fruit juices, Strong/Weak acids, Titration)",
        "Chemical Equilibrium (Ferric-Thiocyanate, Cobalt-Chloride)",
        "Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solutions, Titrations)",
        "Qualitative Analysis (Cation/Anion detection, Organic elements)"
      ]
    },
    "project_assessment": {
      "marks": 4,
      "description": "Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources",
      "suggested_topics": [
        "Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)",
        "Methods of purification of water",
        "Testing hardness/ions in regional drinking water",
        "Foaming capacity of washing soaps",
        "Acidity of tea leaves",
        "Rate of evaporation of different liquids",
        "Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers",
        "Acidity of fruit and vegetable juices"
      ],
      "note": "Projects should involve about 10 periods of work with teacher approval"
    }
  }
}
